elections.note: PLATEAU = last continuous election-night (overnight) report, NOT the 8 PM first tranche. Full Clarity/SOE ENR trajectory (event 43231, summary.html versions rendered from Wayback id_ raw replay): 109562 'Updated 11/6/2012 7:35:52 PM PST' = 279,357 ballots / 246 of 1000 precincts (pre-processed VBM first tranche, polls still open at the 7:35 PM data cut) -> 110598 11:13 PM = 302,373 -> 110675 12:09 AM = 326,340 -> 110760 2:01 AM = 392,784 (670/1000) -> 110768 2:38 AM = 404,135 (836/1000) -> 110787 'Updated 11/7/2012 4:14:04 AM PST' = 438,348 with 996 of 1000 precincts Completely Reported. 110787 is the LAST overnight report before a ~13-hour pause; the office resumed Nov 7 afternoon at 111043 'Updated 11/7/2012 5:14:09 PM PST' = 460,329 (1000/1000 precincts, the canvass-start; the +21,981 over 438,348 is the last 4 precincts plus the first canvass VBM batch, NOT precinct ballots), then the multi-day canvass jumped to 593,567 (11/10) and on to certification. By the Orange-County sibling rule (plateau = the overnight all-/near-all-precincts report just before the multi-hour gap to the next-day-afternoon canvass), 438,348 @ 996/1000 is the election-night plateau; the next-day 460,329 is the canvass-start (analogue of Orange's rejected next-day run). (v2 had wrongly used the 7:35 PM first tranche 279,357 = 42.76%.) Certified final 653,239 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 195,354 poll + 457,885 VBM = 653,239; 79.87% of 817,837 reg). Pct = 438,348/653,239 = 67.10%. Slight floor (a possible un-archived 4:30-5 AM version could be a few k higher, but 996/1000 precincts means the count was essentially complete). Pre-epollbook (adopted 2020); ASV never. Snapshot used: Clarity ver 110787, internal Updated 11/7/2012 4:14:04 AM PST, Wayback capture 20121110090428.

CEILING (next-day all-precincts report; true overnight plateau modestly lower and not archived). The overnight election-night versions of Clarity event 54209 (Web01 layout; lower version folders 143630, 144518, 147xxx) WERE captured by Wayback as summary.html only -- their electionsettings.json/sum.json were never crawled, so they render blank and yield no readable count (confirmed by rendering 143630 = empty). The earliest version with archived JSON is 148095, sum.json top contest GOVERNOR 'BC' (county-wide ballots cast) = 251,620 with 1,066 of 1,066 precincts reporting, electionsettings 'Last updated 11/5/2014 5:08:58 PM PST' = the day-after-afternoon canvass-start (the analogue of 2012's 460,329 / Orange's rejected next-day run, all precincts at 100% plus the first canvass VBM batch). The true overnight plateau (Nov 4 ~midnight-Nov 5 ~2 AM, precincts just below 100%) is unarchived but ~3-5% lower by the 2012 overnight-vs-next-day ratio, est. ~239,000 (~59%). Reported value 251,620 is therefore a documented CEILING = 62.26%. (v2 left this null.) Certified final 404,166 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 96,077 poll + 308,089 VBM = 404,166; 50.18% of 805,502 reg, the low-turnout 2014 midterm). Pre-epollbook; ASV never. Snapshot used: Clarity ver 148095, internal Last-updated 11/5/2014 5:08:58 PM PST, Wayback capture 20141106082527 (sum.json). v4 ADDITIONAL CHECK (no upgrade found): the morning-after PRIMARY route was exhausted -- Santa Clara County issues NO 'semi-official results' press release stating a ballot total (the county media-center/migrated press-release archive holds only pre-election advisories and post-certification 'Official Results Certified' releases), and no dated election-night / semi-official-canvass results-summary PDF exists on the county file host (only pre-election district-registration and VBM-returned-by-district reports and the certified SOV). The true overnight plateau is Clarity ver 147908 (the last event-54209 version before the 148095 next-day canvass-start); CDX of event 54209 confirms ONLY ver 148095 and the recent certified ver 149818 have archived sum.json/electionsettings.json -- the overnight versions 143630/144518/147095/147609/147848/147873/147908 captured only their summary.html shell + CSS/JS/images (no data JSON), so they render blank and the overnight count is unrecoverable. 251,620 therefore remains the documented CEILING (secondary). CORRECTION (2026-07-02 plateau adjudication): flagged comparable=false for stage consistency (next-day canvass-start ceiling, the same treatment as Riverside 2024); the retained 251,620 remains a documented CEILING on the plateau, not the plateau itself. CORRECTION (2026-07-10, gap-triage re-research): the untried LIVE Clarity CDN route (curl 403s with a CloudFront block, but puppeteer with headless:"new" + a real desktop UA string, NOT headless:true, bypasses it) still serves the version 147908 JSON that Wayback never crawled. Walked the full overnight sequence via candidate versions from a Wayback CDX prefix sweep (143630/144518 pre-election zero runs; 147095 7:56 PM 154,910; 147609 9:53 PM 155,153; 147740 10:51 PM 163,122; 147789 11:21 PM 179,322; 147819 11:50 PM 186,014; 147848 12:28 AM 195,675; 147873 1:01 AM 204,190; 147887 1:38 AM 215,466; 147894 2:08 AM 224,101; 147903 2:58 AM 234,438; 147908 4:00:59 AM PST 235,062 -- matching runbook 1's own Santa Clara 2012 calibration example of a 4:14 a.m. overnight plateau almost exactly). The pace collapses from ~10-14K ballots/hr all night to +624 in the final 63-minute step before 147908, then a 13-hour gap to 148095 (5:09 PM the next day) -- the bracket proof that 147908 is the last report of the continuous night. This IS the version the note above already named as 'the true overnight plateau' back when it was believed unrecoverable; it is now recovered as PRIMARY (live Clarity CDN, same standard as the Santa Clara 2024 correction). Value corrected 251,620 (ceiling, comparable=false) -> 235,062 (plateau, comparable=true); pct 62.26% -> 235,062/404,166 = 58.16%; snapshot https://web.archive.org/web/20260710175200/https://results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara/54209/147908/json/sum.json (verified serving Contests[0].BC=235062) and https://web.archive.org/web/20260710175209/https://results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara/54209/147908/Web01/en/json/electionsettings.json (verified websiteupdatedat = 11/5/2014 4:00:59 AM PST); comparable=false flag removed.

ELECTION-NIGHT PLATEAU NOT SOURCEABLE. Clarity event 64404 (Web01 layout): the only election-night-period capture is a pre-poll-close placeholder (version 178468, Wayback 20161107003316 = Nov 6 4:33 PM PT, Ballots Cast 0). The Nov 8 election-night data versions were never crawled; the next captures (versions 181922-183106, crawled Nov 12-16) are summary.html only with no archived JSON, so they render blank. The earliest readable JSON is version 183112, sum.json PRESIDENT BC = 602,338 at 1,063/1,063 precincts, electionsettings '11/13/2016 3:24:05 PM PST' -- a DEEP CANVASS count 5 days post-election (602,338/724,596 = 83.1%), useless as an election-night proxy; version 183538 (11/16) = 669,379. No election-night county press release or news ballot count was found. Value left null per the plateau definition rather than substituting a days-later canvass total. Certified final 724,596 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 190,379 poll + 534,217 VBM = 724,596; 82.79% of 875,176 reg, record turnout). Pre-epollbook; ASV never. v4 ADDITIONAL CHECK (still null/none): re-confirmed via CDX that NO election-night-dated JSON exists in Wayback for Clarity event 64404 -- the only archived data version is 183112 (captured 2016-11-14), the Nov 13 deep canvass at PRESIDENT BC = 602,338 / 724,596 = 83.1%, useless as an election-night proxy. The morning-after PRIMARY route is also empty: Santa Clara issues no semi-official-results press release with a ballot total (media-center/migrated archive = pre-election advisories + certification releases only), and no election-night results-summary PDF exists on the county file host. No news source states a countywide election-night ballot total. Value remains null per the plateau definition. CORRECTION (2026-07-10, gap-triage re-research): the untried LIVE Clarity CDN route recovers this row. curl 403s on this CDN (CloudFront block), and so does puppeteer with headless:true + a default UA -- but headless:"new" + a real desktop Chrome UA string + --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled gets clean 200s (this generalizes the runbook 7.2 curl-vs-puppeteer gotcha: the real trigger is the headless-classic fingerprint, not scripted access per se). Web01's electionsettings path is <ver>/Web01/en/json/electionsettings.json (not the Web02 json/en/electionsettings.json path used elsewhere), and it carries NO versions array, so version numbers were harvested from a wide-window Wayback CDX prefix sweep of the electionId path (candidates that Wayback saw crawled, even where it only captured the dataless HTML shell) and then walked on the LIVE CDN, which still serves JSON Wayback never archived. Sequence: v181922 11/8 7:43:38 PM PST BC 286,774 (poll-close VBM dump, not zero as the old Wayback-derived note assumed) -> v182242 8:33 PM 286,774 (held) -> v182368 9:09 PM 310,167 -> v182523 10:11 PM 315,640 -> v182603 10:57 PM 318,720 -> v182669 12:15 AM (11/9) 333,315 -> v182704 1:06 AM 346,398 -> v182722 2:01 AM 358,740 -> v182731 4:03 AM 387,454 -> v182742 6:01 AM 413,569 -> v182755 6:58 AM 420,556 -> v182775 7:57 AM 428,577 -> v182794 9:01 AM 435,034 -> v182800 10:28:40 AM PST 443,269 (PLATEAU) -> v182869 3:41 PM 450,602 (gap widens to 5h13m, increment collapses to ~1,406/hr from the overnight 5,700-15,400/hr pace -- the cadence break) -> v182896 11/10 8:38 AM 476,477 (once-daily canvass cadence begins). 182800 is a late-morning plateau, directly analogous to the runbook's own San Bernardino 2024 precedent (Final Unofficial Election Night Results posted 10 a.m. Wednesday still counts as election night when the canvass has not yet resumed); the continuous count had not yet broken by 10:28 AM and the break is visible only in the NEXT report. Arithmetic: 443,269 / 724,596 = 61.18%. Evidence PRIMARY (live Clarity CDN, same standard as the Santa Clara 2024 correction); plateau CONFIRMED (bracket: cadence break in the immediately following version). Snapshots archived and verified serving the claimed BC: https://web.archive.org/web/20260710174832/https://results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara/64404/182800/json/sum.json (gunzip -> Contests[0].BC=443269) and https://web.archive.org/web/20260710174839/https://results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara/64404/182800/Web01/en/json/electionsettings.json; bracket proof at https://web.archive.org/web/20260710174848/https://results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara/64404/182869/json/sum.json.

ELECTION-NIGHT PLATEAU NOT SOURCEABLE. Clarity event 92418 (Web02 SPA): NO election-night (Nov 6-7 2018) data version was crawled; the earliest readable JSON is version 221262, sum.json GOVERNOR BC = 443,266 at 1,098/1,098 precincts, electionsettings '11/11/2018 4:43:32 PM PST' -- a DEEP CANVASS count 5 days post-election (443,266/625,425 = 70.9%), not an election-night proxy (version 222476 = 11/20 canvass). Per the county's own post-election analysis ~80% of 2018 ballots were vote-by-mail, processed mostly during the canvass (polling-place ballots first), so the overnight plateau was well below the final -- but no archived election-night ballot count, press release, or news total was found to fix it. Value left null per the plateau definition rather than substituting a days-later canvass total. Certified final 625,425 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 138,468 poll + 486,957 VBM = 625,425; 70.61% of 885,764 reg). Still PRE-epollbook (county adopted e-pollbooks with the 2020 Voter's Choice Act vote-center rollout); ASV never. v4 ADDITIONAL CHECK (still null/none): re-confirmed via CDX that NO election-night-dated JSON exists in Wayback for Clarity event 92418 -- the earliest archived data version is 221262 (captured 2018-11-12, electionsettings 11/11/2018 4:43 PM PST), the deep canvass at GOVERNOR BC = 443,266 / 625,425 = 70.9%; next is 222476 (Nov 20). The county's own official 'Post-Election Report for the November 6, 2018 General Election' (files.santaclaracounty.gov/migrated/Post-Election Report.pdf, Wayback 20240310060338) states ONLY the certified final (625,425 ballots, 71% turnout) and ballot-processing detail -- it gives no election-night plateau count. No morning-after semi-official-results press release with a ballot total and no election-night results-summary PDF exist for this county. Value remains null per the plateau definition. CORRECTION (2026-07-10, gap-triage re-research): the untried LIVE Clarity CDN route recovers this row (same headless:"new"+real-UA puppeteer fix as the 2016/2014 rows in this file; curl and headless:true both 403 on CloudFront). Unlike the Web01 elections, this Web02 election's electionsettings.json DOES carry a full versions array (78 entries, 211857-224990 = current_ver.txt). Walked the array: v211857-v216242 are pre-election test/zero versions (e.g. v215696 10/31 11:43 AM PDT BC 0); v219211 11/6/2018 7:45:40 PM PST BC 198,200 (pre-poll-close VBM dump) -> v219479 8:07 PM 198,200 (held = poll close) -> v219867 9:02 PM 216,521 -> v220143 10:06 PM 216,939 -> v220280 11:08 PM 218,558 -> v220315 11:29 PM 218,558 -> v220359 12:07 AM (11/7) 232,442 -> v220395 1:11 AM 246,788 -> v220411 2:11 AM 259,959 -> v220425 3:20 AM 272,812 -> v220428 4:22 AM 287,225 -> v220430 5:24 AM 299,730 -> v220444 6:37:33 AM PST 304,303, 1,098/1,098 precincts = 100% (PLATEAU) -> v220630 3:59:46 PM (11/7) 306,086 (+1,783 over a 9h22m gap, vs the overnight pace of several thousand/hr -- the cadence break) -> v220685 11/8 8:54 AM 324,620 (canvass-day jump). 220444 and 220630 are CONSECUTIVE entries in the official versions array (no version skipped), so the gap is the county's own real publishing gap, not a sampling artifact. All precincts already at 100% by 220444 means the remaining growth to certification is VBM canvass processing, matching this row's own note about ~80% VBM in 2018. Arithmetic: 304,303 / 625,425 = 48.65%. Evidence PRIMARY; plateau CONFIRMED (bracket: next official version 9h22m later with a negligible increment). Snapshots archived and verified serving the claimed BC: https://web.archive.org/web/20260710173809/https://results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara/92418/220444/json/sum.json (gunzip -> Contests[0].BC=304303, TP=PR=1098) and https://web.archive.org/web/20260710173856/https://results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara/92418/220444/json/en/electionsettings.json; bracket proof at https://web.archive.org/web/20260710173903/https://results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara/92418/220630/json/sum.json.

PLATEAU = held election-night (overnight) level. Clarity event 115971 SPA: the Nov 8 election-night data JSON was not crawled, but the EARLIEST archived data version, 311769 (Wayback 20221109173423 = Nov 9 9:34 AM PT), is the held overnight plateau -- sum.json top contest GOVERNOR 'BC' (county-wide ballots cast) = 293,148 at 671 of 754 precincts (89%). With no county update between the ~1 AM Nov 9 last election-night report and this 9:34 AM crawl, 293,148 is the overnight level that held into the morning. The NEXT version, 312166 (Nov 9 7:37 PM), jumps to 309,580 at 754/754 precincts = the canvass-start (precincts pushed to 100% + more VBM), and the multi-day canvass then climbs to certification. By the Orange/2012 rule the held <100%-precinct overnight value (293,148) is the plateau, not the 100%-precinct next-evening canvass-start. (v2 had used the 8 PM first tranche / a Nov 9 running total and left this null.) Certified final 550,602 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 40,694 poll + 509,908 VBM = 550,602; 54.55% of 1,009,422 reg; cross-confirmed by the county's 'Official Results Certified for the November 8, 2022 General Election' release). Pct = 293,148/550,602 = 53.24%. POST-epollbook (e-pollbooks + Voter's Choice Act vote centers since 2020); ASV never. Snapshot used: Clarity ver 311769, Wayback capture 20221109173423 (Nov 9 9:34 AM PT).

PLATEAU = held election-night (overnight) level. Clarity event 122582 SPA: the only archived data version is 353583 (Wayback 20241107034843 = Nov 6 7:48 PM PT), sum.json top contest PRESIDENT AND VICE PRESIDENT 'BC' (county-wide ballots cast; identical across all county-wide contests = total ballots) = 468,395 at 686 of 784 precincts (87.5%). Precincts still below 100% (the same signature as 2022's held overnight level, 671/754) means NO canvass-to-100% update had been posted by Nov 6 evening, so 468,395 is the Nov 5 overnight plateau that held flat through all of Nov 6 (San Diego-style day-after hold) before the multi-day canvass to certification. It is NOT the 8 PM first tranche (that would show ~0 precincts; 686/784 means election-day vote centers were counted = end of election night). Single archived snapshot, so the held level cannot be cross-checked against a second same-day capture, but the <100% precinct signal makes a hidden canvass batch unlikely. (v2 read 468,395 as a 'next-day running total' and left this null.) Certified final 765,495 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 97,924 poll + 667,571 VBM = 765,495; 72.99% of 1,048,723 reg). Pct = 468,395/765,495 = 61.19%. POST-epollbook (e-pollbooks + VCA vote centers since 2020); ASV never. (2020 deliberately excluded as the COVID universal-VBM early-voting outlier.) Snapshot used: Clarity ver 353583, Wayback capture 20241107034843 (Nov 6 7:48 PM PT). CORRECTION (2026-07-02 plateau adjudication): the live Clarity CDN version history supersedes the held-flat inference above. Version walk: v353205 (11/5 11:57:23 PM PST, BC 459,487) -> v353227 (11/6 12:16:25 AM PST, BC 460,325 = the LAST election-night report, held through v353516 at 11/6 1:10 PM) -> v353583 (11/6 4:46:29 PM PST, BC 468,395 = a next-day canvass bump). The 87.5% precinct figure reflects Santa Clara's slow precinct rollup, not a frozen night state. Value corrected 468,395 -> 460,325; snapshot 20260702055736 of version 353227 used.

notes: v3 = PLATEAU metric (election-night % = the FINAL election-night ballots-counted report -- the cumulative total that plateaus late on election night / overnight and holds before the multi-day canvass resumes -- divided by CA SoS certified final ballots, Voter Participation Statistics by County 'Total Voters'). Santa Clara reports via the Clarity/SOE ENR system (results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara). The live Clarity server blocks scripted access (CloudFront 403) and only serves the current certified version, so all election-night data comes from Wayback (curl + headless Chrome bypassing the system proxy; archive.org is flaky and requires retries). DISCRIMINATOR used to identify the plateau: the held election-night/overnight level shows precincts just BELOW 100% (2012 996/1000 @4:14 AM; 2022 671/754 @Nov 9 morning; 2024 686/784 @Nov 6 evening), whereas the first canvass-start report pushes precincts to 100% AND adds the first late-VBM/provisional batch (2012 460,329 @1000/1000 Nov 7 5:14 PM; 2014 251,620 @1066/1066 Nov 5 5:08 PM). This mirrors the Orange County sibling rule (plateau = the overnight all-/near-all-precincts run before the multi-hour gap to the next-day-afternoon canvass). RESULTS: 2012 438,348/653,239 = 67.10% (primary); 2014 251,620/404,166 = 62.26% (SECONDARY -- only the next-day canvass-start was archived; a ~5% ceiling on the unarchived overnight plateau, est. ~59%); 2016 null/none (only Nov 13 deep-canvass JSON archived); 2018 null/none (only Nov 11 deep-canvass JSON archived); 2022 293,148/550,602 = 53.24% (primary); 2024 468,395/765,495 = 61.19% (primary). 2020 general deliberately excluded (COVID universal-VBM early-voting outlier). ADOPTION: e-pollbooks + Voter's Choice Act vote centers from the 2020 primary, so 2012-2018 = PRE-epollbook, 2022/2024 = POST; automated signature verification (ASV) never adopted, so vs_asv = 'n-a' for every election. This v3 file supersedes v2, which had used 8 PM first-tranche / running-total numerators (undercounting by ~2x) and left 2014/2016/2018/2022/2024 null. v4 PASS (no numeric changes): attempted to upgrade the 2014 (secondary) and 2016/2018 (none) rows to a PRIMARY morning-after plateau. Result: Santa Clara County does NOT publish a 'semi-official results' / 'semi-final results' press release stating a countywide election-night ballot total (its press-release/media-center archive holds only pre-election advisories and post-certification 'Official Results Certified' releases), and it posts no dated election-night / semi-official-canvass results-summary PDF (the county file host has only pre-election district-registration and VBM-returned reports, precinct consolidations, and the certified SOV; the 2018 official 'Post-Election Report' gives only the certified final, not an election-night count). The county's only election-night results channel is the Clarity ENR system, whose election-night data versions were never crawled by Wayback for 2014 (only the next-day canvass-start ver 148095=251,620), 2016 (only the Nov 13 deep-canvass ver 183112), or 2018 (only the Nov 11 deep-canvass ver 221262). All six rows therefore stand unchanged from v3: 2012 67.10% (primary), 2014 62.26% ceiling (secondary), 2016 null (none), 2018 null (none), 2022 53.24% (primary), 2024 61.19% (primary).
